title: hieracium flagelliferum subsp. valsanctae
intro: Hieracium flagelliferum subsp. valsanctae, also known as Valsant's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium flagelliferum subsp. valsanctae are said to include wound healing, immune system support, and the potential treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams for medicinal use.
